title: Search Metadata, Indexing Directives, Canonical Signals, and Language Alternates · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in search discovery and indexability audit record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Extract title and description candidates with cardinality, location, text quality, changed-case, and stakeholder-specific non-ranking claims.
Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
Parse page-level robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by applicable user agent and separate crawling from indexing and serving.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile search metadata, robots directives, canonical signals, language alternates, and indexability non-claims; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Report status, content type, robots directives, canonical signals, authentication, rendered state, and search-engine-specific limits as evidence, not a guaranteed index outcome.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Indexability Decision
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-indexability-decision-1hfhko" that uses case 5764 to reconcile search metadata, robots directives, canonical signals, language alternates, and indexability non-claims. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Indexability Decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Indexability Decision into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Indexability Decision, keep Indexability Decision connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-indexability-decision-1hfhko
# Competency: scraper-indexability-decision.
# Case 5764: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_indexability_decision_1hfhko(pages: int = 36, indexing_decisions: int = 36, canonical_conflicts: int = 4, alternate_sets: int = 6, unsupported_index_guarantees: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1hfhko = "1hfhko-okhfh1-5764"
    assert evidence_variant_1hfhko
    evidence_axes_1hfhko = (
        "authorization1hfhko",
        "url5764",
        "responseokhfh1",
        "tree1hfhko5764",
        "frontier57641hfhko",
        "reportokhfh15764",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1hfhko)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or indexing_decisions < 0:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-count"
    if indexing_decisions != pages:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-reconciliation"
    if canonical_conflicts < 0 or alternate_sets < 1:
        return False, canonical_conflicts, "search-signal-evidence"
    if unsupported_index_guarantees > 0:
        return False, unsupported_index_guarantees, "index-guarantee"
    assert indexing_decisions == pages and unsupported_index_guarantees == 0
    return True, indexing_decisions, "evidence-based-indexability-audit"
